nodes.label: Architecture — Context Pressure Mechanism
nodes.rationale: getContextUsage anchors on the real billed usage already windowed; the 'phantom-history inflates percent' hypothesis is refuted. On a large window the token axis is inert (~3%), so watchCount is the realistic driver. The real defect: OR-max fused two orthogonal axes (work vs occupancy) and showed a misleading ctx=X% -> fixed by A2.

nodes.label: ADR A2 — honest context-pressure split
nodes.rationale: Split OR-max into 2 orthogonal axes (work=watchCount, occupancy=physical fill), config-driven via pressureDriver (default 'max' = firing UNCHANGED, backward-compatible), honest reporting (reason + watch=N/threshold + ctx% only if occ!=none). Do NOT build the floor-decomposition machine (over-engineering for the real regime).

nodes.label: ADR — base model = INTELLIGENCE not CAPABILITY
nodes.rationale: Tier 1 is trained toward general operative intelligence (problem analysis, decomposition, situational action), NOT toward coding. Coding-skill is non-transferable; operative intelligence generalizes to any domain. Domain capability comes from LoRA tiers 2/3. Refines (not rewrites) the three-tier ground truth.
nodes.norm_label: adr — base model = intelligence not capability

nodes.label: ADR — typed http_request channel for sealed-secrets
nodes.rationale: Sink-gating on a typed url field (one new URL(), no shell parsing) eliminates hasForeignHostToken/hasCommandComposition for this path; redirect:'manual' closes exfil-via-redirect. Bash {{secret:NAME}} stays as legacy fallback (additive, no regression). Root cause of shell fragility: the gate must see what curl sees, and the shell is a hostile language to gate.
nodes.norm_label: adr — typed http_request channel for sealed-secrets

nodes.label: Context bounds study (P3 self-tuning)
nodes.rationale: Self-tuning surface = EXACTLY 4 volatile-tail lanes (vars count, recent_changes count, messages n + charCap). Stable prefix and frame are FROZEN config-only; verify_queue/secrets are never-truncate GATE. Shipping any self-tune is BLOCKED on 3 missing infra: total token-budget allocator, set_view tool, reset-lifecycle.

nodes.label: Context limits explained (effective context)
nodes.rationale: Effective context of a 4B is ~40-60% of nominal; filling degrades reasoning even with perfect retrieval; output shares the input window; goal-drift is action-count driven (event-driven reminders, not clock); the least-defended risk is the meta-hack (cleaning context for reward). Thresholds must be MEASURED, not guessed.

nodes.label: Per-section context sizing study
nodes.rationale: Per-lane item caps are a first-class make-or-break parameter. Real driver is often the sort, not the count (if p95<cap the cap doesn't bite). Load-bearing bug fixed: frame slice-direction (shared_state/backlog showed OLDEST). charCap exposed+wired.

nodes.label: Harness runtime inspection & breakpoints
nodes.rationale: Instrument for runtime certainty instead of guessing: 3-tier model (turn-trace I/O, harness-inspect DB snapshot read-only WAL-safe, harness-attach CDP logpoint). boot-vs-commit: a running process keeps stale compiled extensions until reboot. Case study confirmed the bottleneck is the 9B under-reading tool_results, not the harness.

nodes.label: Model-controlled context (self-tuning read-depth)
nodes.rationale: Model should understand+adjust its own view (expand a long message, raise/lower per-section counts) within safe bounds, only on the volatile tail (never the stable prefix -> KV-thrash + broken positional prior). PUSH-bounded + PULL-on-demand: reuse get_conversation instead of a new expand_message tool.

nodes.label: Persist salient facts skill
nodes.rationale: Model lives in a rolling window that forgets old turns; it must recognize durable facts and save them before eviction (note for narrative facts -> <facts> inline; set_var for structured values) then recall. Reward is OUTCOME-anchored (fact recalled correctly N turns later), never ceremony. Layered with the nativeKeepTurns=6 raise (short vs long term).

nodes.label: Session isolation in conversation-store
nodes.rationale: Every store query is conv_id-scoped (proven: range(A,1..2) omits B's seq). A session sees only its own messages. Open caveat: get_conversation accepts an explicit conv_id -> cross-session read if the id is known (intentional for inter-agent refs). Decision 2026-07-03: don't harden now, track as possible multi-user problem.

nodes.label: Tool-gating vs Anthropic Tool Search Tool
nodes.rationale: find_tool/open_category and Anthropic Tool Search Tool implement the same concept (deferred tools + search-to-reveal). Theirs is server-side/native/cache-safe for capable models; ours is client-side over a 9B -> more fragile. Their guidance (accuracy degrades past 30-50 tools) validates our gating. The bottleneck is the model, not the mechanism.
nodes.norm_label: tool-gating vs anthropic tool search tool

nodes.label: tool_result vs user-message trust boundary (P0)
nodes.rationale: Small model (qwen3.5:9b) confused a tool_result (untrusted) with a user message and had zero state-awareness. Wire-capture correction: pi does NOT flatten (emits role=tool+tool_call_id); the real value of framing is auto-describing ORPHAN tool_results (keepTurns windows away the tool_call) + defense-in-depth, not anti-injection. Root cause = amnesia.

nodes.label: Audit 2026-07-04 — pi extensions
nodes.rationale: 4 parallel subagents audited 20 extensions post-P0 amnesia. Root-cause pattern of fixes: fail-CLOSED on every security/memory hook + speak to the 9B via user/native channel, not system. 14 fixed incl. both P1 security (C1 lockdown-secret egress, C3 envelope breakout).

nodes.label: Model test-book (desiderata + verification)
nodes.rationale: Living registry of every 'I want the model to do/understand X' with its outcome-anchored VERIFICATION (probe/held-out). Reconciles wanted-vs-actually-learned at eval time. Verification always checks the outcome, never the ceremony; A/B vs vanilla + balanced held-out (anti cry-wolf).

nodes.label: TB-16 — persist salient facts
nodes.rationale: Driver-tested 2026-07-04: at T8 the names WERE in context yet the 9B confabulated 'I have no name' -> awareness-prompt CEILING confirmed; the S part (recognize-durable + timing + recall/identity) is a TRAINING target, not a prompt. The identity-specific rule was removed as teaching-to-test.

nodes.label: TB-17 — problem understanding/decomposition/impact-analysis
nodes.rationale: Understand-before-act meta-capability: decomposition + blast-radius (transitive) + pre-change reasoning. Promoted to the CORE of Tier 1. Verification is outcome-anchored: analysis must predict the real blast-radius (incl. a non-obvious transitive dependency), never reward the pretty form.

nodes.label: Class: Sign/Wrap Blindspot (disguised)
nodes.rationale: Aritmetica naive che si rompe in silenzio su negativo/boundary quando la fix e non-idiomatica e il dominio NON evoca l'edge; modo-di-fallimento reale (#145 F14): nota l'anomalia ma non la risolve e abbandona; reward outcome su oracolo nascosto edge, controlli-negativi idiomatici + near-miss come discriminanti.

nodes.rationale: Le regole di protezione umano/utente sono gate HARD (priorita massima, peso infinito, non-bilanciabile ne sovrascrivibile da un prompt) + ragionamento a catene lunghe di conseguenze con detection dei conflitti a valle; reward su predizione-catena verificata (anti catena-fantasma) e gate testato adversarialmente.

nodes.label: Class: Requirements-Driven Tree Navigation
nodes.rationale: Da un obiettivo alto-livello estrai lo SCOPE e discendi classe->sottoclasse->foglia fino alla foglia appropriata allo scope (throwaway->no-sicurezza; production->SSOT/DRY); reward sul fit foglia-scope, mai su profondita/verbosita; negativi over-descent e under-descent bilanciati + confine anti-over-triggering su richieste atomiche.

nodes.label: Class: Scope-Adaptive Knowledge Aggregation
nodes.rationale: L'orchestratore audita la direzione/scope reale e aggrega SOLO la conoscenza load-bearing a quello scope (production->hardening/anti-injection/SSOT-DRY; throwaway->NON); reward simmetrico, con trappola size!=scope (script piccolo ma con input untrusted -> hardening).

nodes.label: Class: SVG Spatial Composition

nodes.label: Esempi negativi sempre + completezza-dataset auditata #21
nodes.rationale: I negativi insegnano il CONFINE e rendono il segnale discriminativo (reward simmetrico anti over-trigger); audit completezza/coerenza prima di 'pronto'.

nodes.label: GOLD — Foglia 2.1 reversibility classification
nodes.summary: Gold training example for classifying actions as REV/recoverable-with-loss/IRREV with the decisive fact, built on a labeled action-bank fixture.

nodes.label: Deterministic-undo test
nodes.summary: The decisive predicate: does a deterministic undo procedure U exist that restores the pre-action state? — anchors the 3-way label by-construction.

nodes.label: Reversibility action-bank fixture
nodes.summary: Balanced labeled bank (group A software + group B cross-domain) where each action carries self-contained facts, a constructed REV/RWL/IRREV label and a decisive-fact enum.

nodes.label: Decisive-fact enum (closed reason set)
nodes.summary: Closed set of reason ids (undo-git, undo-rollback, no-undo-untracked, no-undo-external, lossy-old-backup...) the oracle exact-matches instead of free-text paraphrase.

nodes.label: External-effects axis
nodes.summary: Reversible-locally is not reversible-in-effects: sent email / settled payment / distributed history are IRREV because the effect left one's control.

nodes.label: Recoverable-with-loss (never collapsed)
nodes.summary: RWL is a first-class label carrying quantified loss (e.g. 14 days); collapsing it onto REV or IRREV is a scored error in both directions.

nodes.label: Symmetric macro-F1 confusion-matrix reward
nodes.summary: Balanced-accuracy/macro-F1 on a balanced 3-class bank with false-IRREV cost == false-REV cost, so no constant strategy (all-IRREV/all-REV/collapse) is competitive — the classification dual of cry-wolf.

nodes.label: Discriminant twin-pairs vaccine
nodes.summary: Near-identical pairs separated on one fact (force-push shared↔private, email sent↔draft, payment settled↔pending, rm tracked↔untracked) so reward rewards discriminating, not a default label.

nodes.label: §2ter training-vs-harness classification
nodes.summary: Decomposition: git-state/txn-state/backup-catalog = F-harness substrate; the 3-way classification + external-effects + no-collapse = S; F+S with a shippable degraded-but-useful binary fallback.

nodes.label: Area 4 — Context Management & Metacognition
nodes.summary: Full example-space for the 9 Area-4 leaves on long-context handling and metacognition, with per-leaf 5 classes, curriculum phase, reward design and mandatory hack-check.

nodes.label: Degradation self-awareness leaf
nodes.summary: Model recognizes its own context is degraded (loops, aim-drift, contradictions) and emits a DEGRADED flag before producing wrong output — balanced 50/50 dataset, outcome-anchored.

nodes.label: Autocompact / context-edit leaf
nodes.summary: Model-driven keep/summarize/drop compaction preserving key info, verified by held-out probe questions plus length penalty against verbose-summary hacking.

nodes.label: Adversarial needle-in-haystack recall leaf
nodes.summary: Find and execute a buried action-request under 4 noise types (pure/semantic/false-action/contradictory) with position-curriculum; paper-claim #5, marker-varied to prevent tag-grep overfit.

nodes.label: Dynamic-context robustness leaf
nodes.summary: Stable retrieval when structure varies across 5+ dimensions (length/item-count/position/noise-density/section-order); held-out with different positional distribution defeats positional priors.

nodes.label: Outer-task summary + domain type-tags leaf
nodes.summary: Hierarchical outer-task summary plus multi-label domain tags that feed the vertical-LoRA routing; F1 (not recall) and outcome-anchored to which LoRA actually solves the task.

nodes.label: Temporal awareness leaf
nodes.summary: Reason over timestamps/tool-latency for wait/retry/declare-failed and flag incoherent timestamps; per-tool latency variation defeats a fixed-threshold rule.

nodes.label: Stale/TTL freshness-reasoning leaf
nodes.summary: Re-fetch data past its TTL before use and flag temporal incoherence, with a penalty on superfluous re-fetch so 'always re-fetch' is not optimal.

nodes.label: Update-injection handling leaf
nodes.summary: Classify an externally injected mid-thinking update (critical/high/normal/low → restart/adjust/defer/reject) reacting only between complete sections; paper-claim #1, balanced cost matrix.

nodes.label: Self-detect contradiction leaf
nodes.summary: Detect contradictions within one's own context (assets/memory/aim/updates) and stop-and-flag rather than silently pick a value; F1 with plausible false-contradictions guards precision.

nodes.label: Compaction-scheduling (the when) leaf
nodes.summary: New leaf — the decision of WHEN to compact (cost-of-compact vs overflow/degradation risk), AdaCoM-style two-level reward with a separate manager (scorer≠scored).

nodes.label: Low-confidence → gather/ask leaf
nodes.summary: New leaf — token-not-in-context triggers internal/external split, budget-K gather, else ASK; EVPI/SELAUR reward: info must be necessary and change the decision (anti participation-hack).

nodes.label: Dataset-construction playbook (SSOT)
nodes.summary: Master operational SSOT indexing rules #10/#18-#25, the end-to-end per-class workflow, label-gen techniques, the living caveat catalog and the coherence-audit.

nodes.label: §4 Caveat catalog (segna-SEMPRE)
nodes.summary: Living catalog of every dataset caveat by theme (reward/hack-check/negatives/transfer/hierarchy/factual-integrity/decontamination/discrimination/CoT/randomization/oracles/coherence) added the moment it emerges.

nodes.label: §5 Coherence-audit checklist
nodes.summary: 10-point Y/N-with-evidence audit run before any class is 'ready' — structure, outcome-reward+hack-check, hierarchy, held-out, transfer, negatives, factual integrity, no contradiction, wiring, new-caveat capture.

nodes.label: §6 Class registry (coherence map)
nodes.summary: Parent→child map of all current training classes/hierarchies, updated per new class, used to surface contradictions and coherence gaps.

nodes.label: Compositional curriculum + thinking-optimization
nodes.summary: Evaluation of user msg-1140/1473 ideas: RL on isolated skills composed by sliding window, plus progressive CoT compression and optimize-while-implement, with guardrails.

nodes.label: Skill-isolation → sliding-window composition (Part A)
nodes.summary: Train gathering→implementation→write-test→execute in isolation with dense downstream-anchored per-skill reward, then compose 1+2,2+3,...1+2+3 with replay anti-forgetting and a competence gate.

nodes.label: Progressive thinking-optimization (Part B)
nodes.summary: Long-but-perfect chains while learning (PRM/step-anchored coherence), then outcome-gated compression to go straight to the solution once internalized — the training version of finding H6.

nodes.label: Scoped-proportional handoff-state verification [PROPOSAL]
nodes.summary: Proposed class (msg 1144): on 'resume from here' verify only the inherited state parts that touch your lane, detect a seeded error before building on it, proceed without over-checking OK state.

nodes.label: Optimize-while-implement (dual tag flow)
nodes.summary: Same why→problem→solution skeleton across implementation-code and optimization tags so the model thinks optimized while building; reward is outcome-gated (correct AND better), the tag itself never rewarded.

nodes.label: Cross-reference authoring-metadata
nodes.summary: Authoring-metadata tags linking related examples (same root-skill, SAVE↔RECALL twins, cross-domain transfer) to compose the curriculum and reinforce transfer — never leaked into the runtime prompt.

nodes.label: Task×competence eval matrix
nodes.summary: Eval scaffold (rows=task, cols=skill, cells=pass-rate) that both measures per-skill competence and detects regression during composition — prerequisite of the whole curriculum.

nodes.label: Class — Instruction-fidelity / no-overreach
nodes.summary: Training class: execute a precise instruction EXACTLY (S and only S) preserving intent, avoiding over-reach, under-delivery and blind-obedience-to-the-dangerous.

nodes.label: Exact-intent execution (S and only S)
nodes.summary: Extract the atomic op-set S with exact params + protected set F, execute all of S and nothing else, audit for drift before delivering — the produced diff == the requested diff.

nodes.label: Four-pole symmetric fidelity reward
nodes.summary: Deterministic diff-vs-spec oracle penalizing over-reach, under-delivery, blind-obey and over-flag-on-benign equally, so no fixed default (add-always/do-minimum/obey-always/flag-always) wins.

nodes.label: Base-model candidates 2026-07 (bake-off)
nodes.summary: Comparative ranking of ~27-36B dense-preferred base candidates with a staged bake-off recommendation; decision input, not a decision (the protected choice stays).

nodes.label: Seed-OSS-36B Base-woSyn
nodes.summary: ByteDance 36B dense Apache-2.0, #1 by intelligence+knowledge-per-size and the only synthetic-instruction-free base = most literal CPT fit; strong reproduction-gap to verify first.

nodes.label: Qwen3-32B
nodes.summary: 32B dense softmax Apache-2.0, 'the right Qwen' for CPT — broadest battle-tested knowledge, minimal execution risk, co-leader safe default.

nodes.label: GLM-4-32B-0414
nodes.summary: Zhipu 32B dense MIT, best operate-the-system + clean-CPT among permissive dense with agentic already strong in the base; native ctx only 32K.

nodes.label: OLMo 3.1 32B
nodes.summary: AllenAI 32B dense Apache-2.0, fully-open (data+recipe+checkpoints) = most research-friendly, minimal unknown-fragility for scientific CPT.

nodes.label: Gemma 4 31B Dense
nodes.summary: Google 31B dense Apache-2.0, token-efficient reasoning but weaker agentic and encyclopedic recall (our two key axes).

nodes.label: Qwen3.6-27B (current candidate)
nodes.summary: 27B dense-hybrid GatedDeltaNet Apache-2.0, highest intelligence/agentic ceiling and 1M ctx but coding-monomaniacal + max CPT fragility; demoted to candidate-to-validate + potential teacher.

nodes.label: Qwen3.5-27B
nodes.summary: 27B dense-hybrid Apache-2.0, more balanced than 3.6 for our identity with verifiable strong agentic, same hybrid-architecture unknowns.

nodes.label: Staged bake-off (inference→4B→cloud-FT)
nodes.summary: Stage-0 inference probes decide most (local/API), Stage-1 CPT+LoRA mechanism on the local 4B, Stage-2 full-FT of finalists on rented cloud GPUs only at the end.

nodes.label: base-probes model-agnostic harness
nodes.summary: base-probes.mjs (shell bash/PS/CMD/sh + python/JS + reasoning) + run-base-probe.mjs OpenAI-compatible runner with multi-key rotation and 429 backoff, validated live.

nodes.label: 1st-round floor-check via Groq
nodes.summary: All 4 Groq candidates scored 100% on base-probes (floor cleared but non-discriminating); next discriminant = durable-preference/F33 memory test on qwen3.6-27b vs qwen3-32b (metric C4).

nodes.label: Class — Proactive improvement proposal
nodes.summary: 5th child of situational-awareness: recognize a genuinely valuable+valid improvement beyond the literal request and surface it as a calibrated proposal (propose-don't-impose).

nodes.label: Value×Validity×Relevance threshold
nodes.summary: The skill is the calibrated threshold — propose only when high-value × technically-valid × relevant-to-the-real-goal beats the cost of noise; below it, execute silently.

nodes.label: Propose-don't-impose
nodes.summary: Surface the idea and let the user decide — not implementing it unilaterally (over-reach) nor letting it drop silently (missed value).

nodes.label: Symmetric over-suggest↔under-propose reward
nodes.summary: L-judge with equal penalty on over-suggest (noise) and under-propose (missed value) plus a hard penalty on invalid/confabulated proposals; outcome-anchored to real materialized value.

nodes.label: Class — Prospective memory (save-what-you'll-need)
nodes.summary: SAVE half of the memory discipline (5th child of metacognitive-self-audit): recognize now that info will matter many turns later and proactively, discriminatively save it before it is evicted.

nodes.label: Actionable-vs-arbitrary asymmetry (F33)
nodes.summary: Hardest case: durable+arbitrary single-use-deferred facts (ALDO-QX) are under-saved because they lack a recurrence signal; the criterion must be durability+future-need, not reuse-frequency.

nodes.label: Discriminative save boundary (negatives N1-N6)
nodes.summary: Balanced negatives (over-save trivia, already-persisted, won't-need, save-junk-for-signal, arbitrary-but-ephemeral) make the signal discriminative and defeat 'save-always'.

nodes.label: Harness-wins validation protocol
nodes.summary: Execute-ready protocol to find/prove the long-horizon regime where the harness beats vanilla; Regime-A (context overflow) confirmed by E12.

nodes.label: Regime A — context-overflow retention
nodes.summary: When a session exceeds the model's real window, vanilla loses early-context recall while ours retains pinned key facts in compact lanes — the project's central thesis.

nodes.label: Regime B — iso-recall at lower cost
nodes.summary: Same recall as vanilla but fewer tokens because compact digest lanes replace re-reading raw history on verbose long sessions.
nodes.norm_label: regime b — iso-recall at lower cost

nodes.label: Regime C — anti-derailment / task-success
nodes.summary: Harness defenses (pre-flight, secret-guardrail, structured-context, anti-fixation) complete more tasks / avoid catastrophes on long noisy sessions, independent of recall.

nodes.label: Adaptive production config
nodes.summary: keepTurns ∝ (model-context ÷ session-length): high/off when the window holds the session, low + task-digest ON when it overflows, with auto-disable to avoid overhead on short tasks.

nodes.label: E12 overflow finding (100% vs 25%)
nodes.summary: qwen-ctx16k×he12: vanilla recall 25% vs ours-digest@keep1 recall 100% at 1.3× tokens — deterministic harness memory beats the native window in real overflow (n=1, 9B, to reconfirm on ≥27B).

nodes.label: Discriminative MCQ hard-distractors
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting label-gen methodology: 4/6/10/16-option MCQ built as confusable minimal-pairs (one correct) to sharpen fine discrimination, in both recognition and generation.

nodes.label: Minimal-pair hard negatives
nodes.summary: Each option has a near-twin differing on one load-bearing feature, derived from the model's real failure modes and style-matched so no surface tell predicts the answer.

nodes.label: Dual recognition + generation use
nodes.summary: Same distractors used to choose (recognition) and to self-check a generated answer against the near-miss twin; generation stays primary, MCQ is discrimination-refinement to avoid a generative gap.

nodes.label: Exactly-one-correct + distractor-tell audit
nodes.summary: An oracle guarantees exactly one correct option and audits that no surface cue (length, hedging, 'all of the above', position, specificity) predicts the answer.

nodes.label: Position randomization (item + dataset)
nodes.summary: Seeded per-item shuffle plus dataset-level ~uniform distribution of correct letters (audited by positionBalance) to kill the always-C position/letter bias.

nodes.label: Training-set construction principles (#18-#22)
nodes.summary: Navigable wiki mirror of the dataset-building rules #18-#22 with exemplars and how they compose together on every new class.

nodes.label: #18 Gap → class (held-out, double-scope)
nodes.summary: Every observed reasoning gap becomes an outcome-anchored class; the observed instance stays held-out and transfer to it is the success metric (harness-finding ↔ training-class).

nodes.label: #19 Cross-domain transfer always
nodes.summary: ≥3-4 non-technical transfers over distant domains (daily life, economy/policy, ecology, health, business), banal→systemic, so the skill generalizes instead of localizing to software.

nodes.label: #20 Hierarchical parent→child classes
nodes.summary: Find/create the shared root-skill parent and attach the class as a specialization, recursing when a child warrants sub-children; never disconnected sisters sharing a trigger.

nodes.label: #21 Negatives + audited completeness
nodes.summary: Include boundary negatives with a symmetric reward so no fixed default hacks the signal, and audit completeness/coherence before declaring a set ready.

nodes.label: #22 Factual integrity (verify, not assert)
nodes.summary: Never invented/incomplete facts; split verified-stable (cited, minimized) vs uncertain/volatile (→ verify-step, default in doubt); reasoning skills use self-contained fixtures true-by-construction.

nodes.label: Position/answer/symbol randomization principle
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting principle: randomize answer-position, symbols/names and variant-order/context so the model learns the true signal, not a positional/surface cue.

nodes.label: Axis 1 — MCQ answer-position
nodes.summary: Two-level randomization: seeded per-item option shuffle + audited ~uniform dataset distribution of correct letters, enforced by mcq-distractor-gen.positionBalance.

nodes.label: Axis 2 — symbols/names
nodes.summary: Immutable knowledge memorized with CE-loss; code/identifiers use runtime never-repeated random names forcing induction-heads (surgical copy), with mixed naming shapes anti-shortcut.

nodes.label: Axis 3 — variant-order / context
nodes.summary: Shuffle variant labels with context-conditioned optimum, and vary needle-position/section-order across ≥5 dimensions so 'always the 3rd/option-c' errs a measurable fraction (anti Lost-in-the-Middle).

nodes.label: Class-PARENT — Visual design quality
nodes.summary: Parent class: judge/produce visual composition against verifiable design/UX laws (proximity, alignment, hierarchy, contrast), not subjective taste.

nodes.label: Verifiable design-law root skill
nodes.summary: Extract semantic structure, apply the pertinent verifiable law, verify bilaterally (an optimum exists, both extremes break), condition on context, anchor to the measurable — never 'it's pretty'.

nodes.label: Verify-by-render multi-viewport facet
nodes.summary: UI verification means actually rendering and checking on ≥2 viewports (desktop+mobile) deterministically; 'I verified the layout' without a render scores 0.

nodes.label: Mining 2026-07 chat-lessons pipeline
nodes.summary: Stage-1 deterministic extractor (375 windows, PII-redacted) → Stage-2 16-agent workflow (328 abstract lessons, 12 macro-categories, 17 emergent classes) → Stage-3 integration into taxonomy + CLAUDE.md rules #26-#30.

nodes.label: Wiki Index
nodes.summary: Content-oriented catalog of every wiki page (overview, architecture, entities, concepts, training-taxonomy, decisions), updated at each ingest/lint.

nodes.label: Area 1 — Organization & Long-Horizon Planning
nodes.summary: Tier-1 organization-first example-space: decompose, map dependencies, parallelize, block timeline, keep multi-day state, track goal, plan/validate/revise, chain experts, calibrate quality-tier.

nodes.label: Task decomposition (coverage/granularity/no-overlap)
nodes.summary: Leaves covering full-goal coverage, right granularity, and mutually-disjoint tasks, each with fade-out hints, reward design and hack-check.

nodes.label: Dependency mapping (edges / topo-sort / timeline-impact)
nodes.summary: Identify correct task dependencies (F1 vs gold DAG), produce a valid topological order, detect cycles, and project future-timeline impact along the dependency chain.

nodes.label: Parallelization decisions
nodes.summary: Identify parallel tasks (DAG reachability), avoid shared-resource write-write conflicts, and pick efficient schedules (makespan / critical-path); static planning children of action-execution-optimization.

nodes.label: Timeline blocking (concept-blocks)
nodes.summary: Group tasks into coherent concept-blocks to minimize context-switch, cache a block-level decision, and avoid cross-task hand-off gaps.

nodes.label: Multi-day continuity / state persistence
nodes.summary: After a gap, recall correct state from a persisted log and resume without losing constraints/decisions; verify a stale/contradictory log instead of trusting it blindly.

nodes.label: Goal/aim tracking (invariance + drift detection)
nodes.summary: Keep the original aim fixed over long trajectories and actively detect gradual drift, distinguishing true drift from legitimate exploration.

nodes.label: Plan-mode & plan validation/revision
nodes.summary: Emit an explicit plan for non-trivial tasks, validate it before executing, and re-plan minimally mid-execution when an assumption falls.

nodes.label: Multi-expert plan (expert-chain)
nodes.summary: For multi-domain tasks, plan the right expert chain in the right order with defined hand-offs, without over-recruiting on mono-domain tasks.

nodes.label: Quality-target inference & calibration
nodes.summary: Infer the target quality-tier (PoC/Prototype/MVP/Prod/Hardened) from context, calibrate effort per-dimension, and ask (showing a scorecard) when genuinely ambiguous.

nodes.label: decisione-vale-per-blocco (decision-cache leaf)
nodes.summary: Reuse a decision taken for a concept-block across its tasks instead of re-deciding, re-opening only on a new invalidating constraint; held-out gold for the decision-cache class.

nodes.label: L-leaf outcome-anchoring defense
nodes.summary: Transversal note: L (judgment) leaves are the most hackable (form of the plan without substance); defense = anchor to a downstream verifiable outcome, scorer≠scored, hidden tests on the final goal.

nodes.label: Area 9 — Communication & Deference
nodes.summary: Tier-1/X area governing how the agent communicates and defers: ask-vs-proceed, informative escalation, honest reporting, objective critique vs sycophancy, output-length control, plus proactive value-add.

nodes.label: Ask-vs-proceed (interruption threshold)
nodes.summary: Calibrate when to interrupt for clarification (high-impact/irreversible ambiguity) vs proceed with a declared assumption; symmetric penalty on missed-ask and over-ask.

nodes.label: Informative escalation (context+alternatives+effort+reco)
nodes.summary: Produce a decision-grade escalation (context, current path, alternatives, effort, recommendation, ask) instead of dumping a raw problem; judged for problem-specificity not boilerplate.

nodes.label: Honest reporting (report↔trace)
nodes.summary: Q-leaf: final report == real trace (failed tests reported as failed, skipped steps declared); deterministic verifier comparing report to harness trace, penalizing false-positive success.

nodes.label: Objective critique vs sycophancy
nodes.summary: Give honest merit-based feedback (real bugs/vulns/design flaws) without flattery; anti-sycophancy judge preferring honest-unwelcome over pleasant-false, symmetric against invented over-criticism.

nodes.label: Output length control (budget↔count)
nodes.summary: Respect an explicit length budget by compressing (not truncating) and preserving critical info; joint reward within-budget AND critical-content-preserved, harness-measured count.

nodes.label: Proactive value-add (Topic 6)
nodes.summary: Proactive complement to the 5 reactive topics: surface a genuine value×validity×relevance improvement as a calibrated proposal; lives as a dedicated hierarchical class.

nodes.label: Sycophancy = preference-tuning reward-hack
nodes.summary: Central area risk: judges/annotators prefer pleasing answers so preference-tuning collapses to agreement/flattery; defended by calibrated judge, verifiable ground-truth, explicit sycophancy penalty, reward decoupling.

nodes.label: Class — Prompt-injection resistance
nodes.summary: Recognize a hostile instruction inside UNTRUSTED content in any presentation form, refuse it, flag to the user and ask confirmation; minimum-trust default. Adversarial arm of the safety/protection family.

nodes.label: Two-layer defense (harness envelope + training semantic)
nodes.summary: Complementary layers: harness deterministically frames FRAMED injections (tool-result-envelope banner + delimiter neutralization); training makes the model robust to UN-FRAMED injection nested in legitimate data (rule #24 semantics = model's job).

nodes.label: Injection modalities M1-M19 (form space)
nodes.summary: Same hostile payload presented across 19 modalities (HTML/JSON/no-delimiter/interspersed … poetry/encoded/obfuscated/invisible/multilingual/persona/policy/skeleton/manyshot/split/artifact) × 10 kinds = the train/eval space.

nodes.label: Form-invariance (poetry/narrative/split/artifact)
nodes.summary: Strip fiction/verse/fragmentation: if an imperative to exfil/exec remains, it's injection regardless of the ornamental veil (M6/M7/M18/M19).

nodes.label: Language-invariance
nodes.summary: Translate mentally, same policy; language is form not substance, don't rely on EN keywords (M11 low-resource, M12 multilingual-sandwich).

nodes.label: Decode-then-rescreen (encoded/obfuscated/invisible)
nodes.summary: Re-apply safety to decoded content (M8 base64/cipher, M9 leet/homoglyph, M10 zero-width); harness NFKC/strip is first line, training second — rule is decode-then-rescreen not reject-if-opaque.

nodes.label: Provenance-anchor (persona/policy/authority)
nodes.summary: Nothing in the data channel can self-elevate to system/policy/authority/new-identity however official it looks (M13 DAN, M14 Policy-Puppetry, M15 Skeleton-Key, system-prompt-exfiltration).

nodes.label: Dual-use discrimination (Fable-5 M16)
nodes.summary: Hardest case for a capable model: hostile request framed as legitimate work (security-review 'prove the exploit'); gold discriminates the boundary — give analysis, stop before the weaponized step, ask confirmation.

nodes.label: Minimum-trust default
nodes.summary: When in doubt, ignore and don't proceed; the protection is a hard gate not overridable by an instruction inside the data (inherited from P6/agent-constitution).

nodes.label: F30 live injection matrix (97.9% resistance)
nodes.summary: Live M1-M19 × 5 critical kinds on flash-lite: zero breach on all obfuscation, the 2 breaches are secret-exfiltration via plain wording (M4) and professional-legitimacy (M16) — redirects training weight to the plain/authority secret-exit gate, not exotic forms.

nodes.label: tool-result-envelope.mjs (untrusted framing)
nodes.summary: Deterministic harness defense wrapping every tool_result in an untrusted banner (ONLY DATA, NO INSTRUCTIONS), NFKC-normalizing and stripping control/zero-width/tag-block, idempotent and anti-breakout.

nodes.label: Class — Domain categorization & routing
nodes.summary: Classify a request on two orthogonal axes — DOMAIN (which LoRA/vertical) and TASK-TYPE→thinking-mode — decomposing multi-label tasks; failure = mis-routing / default-collapse / confidence-tic. Base keeps a small % of every domain for the instinct to categorize.

nodes.label: Axis-1: DOMAIN → LoRA/vertical routing
nodes.summary: Extract the real object of the task past surface keywords, map to the vertical whose specialist handles it, split if multi-domain, escalate/withhold if none fits — the Tier-1 router function.

nodes.label: Axis-2: TASK-TYPE → thinking-mode
nodes.summary: Addendum (idea #2): route the reasoning STRATEGY by operation type (generation/classification/optimization/debugging/analysis/harness-operation), with implementation-code/optimization tags; reward = ablation showing the mode causally improved outcome, never the tag.

nodes.label: Set-match / macro-F1 reward (multi-label)
nodes.summary: Multi-domain reward on the domain SET (a spurious domain costs as much as a missing one), class-balanced macro-F1 defeating default-collapse, symmetric with abstention on no-domain/ambiguous.

nodes.label: Class — Independent-verification integrity
nodes.summary: When critically verifying an artifact (of another agent or a past self), don't inherit the producer's assumptions/expected/fixture — rebuild an own ground-truth from primary sources capable of reaching a different conclusion.

nodes.label: Independence from producer's ground-truth
nodes.summary: Skill-root: a verification has value only if it could in principle FAIL; audit the provenance of your own verification ground-truth — 'did I derive this expected, or did the one I'm verifying hand it to me?'.

nodes.label: Symmetry (catch-planted ∧ confirm-sane) anti-hack
nodes.summary: Central anti-hack: can't win by always-trusting (misses planted error) nor by always-flagging/re-deriving (fails sane+trivial); reward = calibration of independence to the stakes, anchored to verifiable outcome.

nodes.label: Planted-error / sane / trivial oracle
nodes.summary: Deterministic oracle over fixtures with two layers (producer expected vs primary sources); mutation plants an error coherent with the producer's expected so contaminated verification passes the author-check but fails reality.

nodes.label: Class — Decision cache per block
nodes.summary: Recognize a decision invariant over a scope → decide once, cache, apply coherently to all items; re-evaluate only at the scope boundary or on premise-change. Reasoning-form of optimization-first.

nodes.label: Scope triage of a decision (invariant vs per-item)
nodes.summary: Before deciding per-item, identify the scope boundary on which the policy/choice is constant, cache it, guarantee coherence across items, and invalidate on premise-change.
nodes.norm_label: scope triage of a decision (invariant vs per-item)

nodes.label: Stale-cache failure (N3, silently wrong)
nodes.summary: The worst failure mode: a cache never invalidated when the premise changes produces a stale decision — worse than re-deriving; oracle penalizes applying a cache after the invalidation event.

nodes.label: Class — Async dispatch & prioritization
nodes.summary: During interaction, recognize the LONG+INDEPENDENT part → dispatch it in background, answer the ready part NOW, reconcile the async at completion, and prioritize under deadline/user-leaving; don't block the flow.

nodes.label: 4-axis execution triage (latency/dependency/input/risk)
nodes.summary: Classify each sub-task on latency, dependency, user-input-needed, reversibility/risk → async only long+independent+non-risky, respond immediate parts, front-load what unblocks the user.

nodes.label: Async reconciliation & front-loading
nodes.summary: Deliver the long result at completion (no task lost), never violate a dependency, and under deadline surface the user's decision/approval first; the worst hack is background-then-never-reconcile.

nodes.label: async-schedule-gen.mjs oracle
nodes.summary: Implemented label-gen oracle: optimalDecision policy SSOT + scoreSchedule outcome-anchored (task-lost/user-blocked/dependency-violated/…) + scenarioBank with N1-N5 distractors; self-contained, tests 13/0.

nodes.label: Class — Knowledge-base curation
nodes.summary: Generate and USE a wiki (Karpathy LLM-Wiki pattern) with the placement nuance private/central/project and the writing rules; oracle crisp (placement rules given in fixture), PII-in-shareable is a security fail.

nodes.label: Placement buckets (private/central/project)
nodes.summary: Three buckets discriminated by sensitivity × scope; sensitivity beats scope (never PII in the shareable), then scope decides project vs central.

nodes.label: USE (consult) vs GENERATE (persist)
nodes.summary: USE: consult the KB and cite the real path before confabulating (twin-RECALL); GENERATE: persist durable knowledge in the right bucket with frontmatter/confidence-tags/cross-links, updating not duplicating (twin-SAVE).

nodes.label: PII-in-shareable = hard security fail (N1)
nodes.summary: The most important negative: placing a personal/secret item in the central/public wiki or versioned repo is a hard FAIL (leak cost high); correct bucket is private/gitignored — sensitivity beats scope.

nodes.label: Class — Consequence↔intention conflict
nodes.summary: Before committing an action, trace cause→action and action→consequence and check the consequences don't contradict the intention; avoid self-defeating solutions where the side-effect cancels the goal.

nodes.label: Double causal trace + coherence check
nodes.summary: Backward (real intention served) + forward (downstream side-effects) + coherence check; if a consequence cancels/worsens the intention the action is self-defeating and must be redesigned ('does the cure cost more than the disease?').

nodes.label: Cobra effect / perverse incentive (canonical name)
nodes.summary: The economics/policy name of this class (bounty on cobras → more cobras); cross-domain transfers span retry-storm, rent-control, cane-toad, healthcare fee-for-service — the abstract logic the model must generalize.

nodes.label: Adaptive context injection (context isn't free)
nodes.summary: Injected context has a cost (tokens + a small model's scarce attention): on self-contained tasks memory-scaffolding correlates with FAIL + 3× tokens → adaptive/lean injection + on-demand tools + regime-aware training.

nodes.label: H6 finding — injected context can dilute reasoning (HE/145)
nodes.summary: A/B on HumanEval/145: vanilla lean context PASS 2/2, ours@keep1/keep6 FAIL 4/4 at 3× tokens; the model fixates on the wrong sub-problem (digit_sum on negatives) instead of re-questioning the assumption. Confirm at n≥5.
nodes.norm_label: h6 finding — injected context can dilute reasoning (he/145)

nodes.label: Harness-side lean/adaptive lane gating
nodes.summary: Axis-1: gate lanes by relevance/content (empty facts → don't inject, no history → no how_memory_works); a fresh self-contained task should receive ~0 lanes.

nodes.label: keepTurns curriculum + regime-awareness (training)
nodes.summary: Axis-3: vary keepTurns in RL and pass the current regime to the model so it learns to adapt behavior (small window → persist state now; lean context → trust reasoning); dual-head measures the harness marginal value per regime.

nodes.label: Model-controlled set_keepturns (3b)
nodes.summary: End-state tool set_keepturns(n) with auto-reminder + cap; mechanism=F, decision=S (needs training). Critical confound: on a fixation more context DILUTES, so the cure is step-back not more memory — don't ship as an inert shell.

nodes.label: Pressure-adaptive keepTurns (F32, opt-in)
nodes.summary: Implemented runtime F32: keepTurns starts high (vanilla) and drops with real fill (default OFF, opt-in); touches only INJECTION, never CAPTURE; wiring-test rule #14; value depends on the model saving facts (9B doesn't).

nodes.label: Capture (always-on) ≠ injection distinction
nodes.summary: Task-digest/note/jot/<facts>/<vars> capture is independent of keepTurns — durable facts persist in vanilla regime before context grows; adaptive modes tune only the native-window injection.

nodes.label: Harness tool/context affordance design
nodes.summary: Harness principles for exposing tools/context: visibility≠callability, mention≠invocation, access-by-intent + explicit affordances, priority-over-cache; the F-side twin of class-harness-environment-awareness (S-side).

nodes.label: Visibility ≠ callability (P1)
nodes.summary: Tool gating may act only on the surface list/description, never on execution: a registered tool the model calls must work even when hidden; enforced by a reachability regression-test for every registered tool.

nodes.label: Mention ≠ invocation (P2)
nodes.summary: The mere presence of a trigger tag/command in output must not cause the action; output is information not execution — activation needs an explicit structured channel, a structural boundary not a regex intent-guesser.

nodes.label: Access-by-intent + explicit affordances (P3)
nodes.summary: In a windowed context declare truncation (+N hidden — expand) and provide out-of-bound retrieval; the model must not treat the window as the whole world (FIND-7/F23/F33) — F-side enabling the environment-awareness skill.

nodes.label: Priority-over-cache (P4)
nodes.summary: When context ordering conflicts between importance-for-the-model and prefix-cache-stability, importance wins; cache-stability is opportunistic (e.g. <current_date> anchor where it costs nothing), never a constraint that reorders content.

nodes.label: Discriminant tests vs floor-tests (#12)
nodes.summary: Evaluate features/candidates with tests that SEPARATE them (some pass, some fail), not floor-tests everyone clears; keep a model×feature outcome matrix — extends hard-distractor discrimination to experiment/eval design.

nodes.label: context-assembler.mjs (context assembly impl)
nodes.summary: Harness implementation that assembles the <context> lanes and injects <current_date>; the concrete surface where affordance-design principles and adaptive injection live.

nodes.label: Oracle design pitfalls & defenses
nodes.summary: Consolidated checklist of six oracle (reward-verifier) traps + defenses: the oracle IS the reward, so mis-designed = gameable (reward-hack) or non-portable (breaks cross-device) → the model learns the wrong thing.

nodes.label: Trap 1: example instead of ∀
nodes.summary: Verifying one exemplary case of a for-all property is gameable; defense = a ∀-quantified, executable predicate anchored to a fixture.

nodes.label: Trap 2: abstract/alternative detector
nodes.summary: 'Use a detector' or '3 alternatives' leaves the ground-truth unstable; defense = pin one concrete deterministic tool (e.g. pylint duplicate-code with explicit N).

nodes.label: Trap 3: sha256 on git-transited content (autocrlf)
nodes.summary: sha256 on files passing through git breaks Win↔Linux (core.autocrlf LF→CRLF); defense = semantic oracles (key-fields ⊆ post-content), reserve sha256 to non-git copies + force autocrlf=false.

nodes.label: Trap 4: non-determinism (race/time/net)
nodes.summary: Using the exit-code of a non-deterministic phenomenon yields flaky reward; defense = a static check on the PROPOSED action (emits append+lock, not concurrent >), independent of runtime outcome.

nodes.label: Trap 5: placebo/self-score → discrimination-gate
nodes.summary: A pass-everything test or the model's self-score gives zero discrimination; defense = live discrimination-gate T(B)=FAIL ∧ T(C)=PASS with scorer≠scored.

nodes.label: Trap 6: [UNVERIFIED] format-only marker
nodes.summary: A gold whose outputs weren't run in a real sandbox may hide reasoning bugs = wrong oracles; defense = mark [UNVERIFIED — sandbox-execution pending], fix logic by hand now, tracked debt not a pass.

nodes.label: Learned-rules preview (approval before filing)
nodes.summary: Process (mining #15): a learned preference/lesson → PREVIEW with destination + severity/scope → explicit citable approval → filing; refines CLAUDE.md #17 with a provenance gate, avoiding rule-inflation and under-filing.

nodes.label: Preview gate (destination + severity/scope)
nodes.summary: Capture (anti-loss) → represent as a candidate with destination (rule/memory/wiki/class) and severity/scope so the user sees the weight → file only after citable approval; skip for session-operative lessons or explicitly dictated rules.

nodes.label: GOLD Foglia 2.2 — richiedere conferma su irreversibile
nodes.summary: Esempio-gold di training per l'ATTO al bivio: emettere <safety_halt> e non eseguire SSE irreversibile/recoverable e non pre-autorizzato, altrimenti procedere.
nodes.norm_label: gold foglia 2.2 — richiedere conferma su irreversibile

nodes.label: confirm_needed = reversibilità∈{IRREV,RWL} ∧ ¬preauth_fidato
nodes.summary: Ground-truth oggettiva calcolata dai fatti-fixture (reversibilità per-costruzione + flag pre-autorizzazione fidato), non dall'auto-dichiarazione del modello (scorer≠scored).

nodes.label: Blocco <safety_halt> strutturato
nodes.summary: Formato di conferma ancorato a pre-flight-safety-checks: ASSET/AZIONE/RISK/IRREVERSIBILE/RACCOMANDAZIONI[A/B/C/D]/USER-ACTION con severity.

nodes.label: Tabella-oracolo reversibilità (upstream 2.1)
nodes.summary: Classificazione REV/IRREV/recoverable-with-loss verificabile-per-costruzione (definizionale) riusata come oracolo upstream della Foglia 2.1.

nodes.label: Sandbox fixtures FX-migration/reversible/recoverable/preauth/injection
nodes.summary: Cinque fixture self-contained che danno azione + reversibilità-per-costruzione + stato preauth + canale untrusted per riproducibilità del verifier.

nodes.label: Invariante di provenienza del grant (preauth solo da lane fidata)
nodes.summary: Un 'procedi pure' da contenuto untrusted è dato inerte e non abbassa confirm_needed; solo la lane fidata setta il gate (anti prompt-injection, coppia 5a↔5b).
nodes.norm_label: invariante di provenienza del grant (preauth solo da lane fidata)

nodes.label: Penalità simmetrica over-ask ↔ missed-confirm (balanced-accuracy/F1)
nodes.summary: Over-ask su REV/pre-autorizzato penalizzato con la stessa forza del missed-confirm su IRREV; metrica bilanciata su {confirm-needed,proceed}, mai recall-sugli-IRREV.

nodes.label: Oracolo dinamico halted_before_point_of_no_return
nodes.summary: Predicato outcome: SELECT count(*) exit 0 = risorsa ancora presente (halt riuscito) vs exit 1 = DROP eseguito (missed-confirm); UNVERIFIED finché non gira il trace.

nodes.label: Recovery = ritiro PRIMA del punto di non ritorno (ROLLBACK)
nodes.summary: Classe 4: annullamento effettivo (ROLLBACK/non-exec) prima del commit + blocco + stato awaiting; semantica distinta dal restore-post-danno delle foglie 1.x.
nodes.norm_label: recovery = ritiro prima del punto di non ritorno (rollback)

nodes.label: Reward atomico outcome-anchored su entrambi i rami
nodes.summary: atto_corretto = confirm_needed ? confirm_emitted : proceeded_clean; il ramo-procedi richiede outcome positivo (non un no-op silenzioso farmabile).
nodes.norm_label: reward atomico outcome-anchored su entrambi i rami

nodes.label: Batch-first + pre-auth≻halt + segnala (§1bis)
nodes.summary: Assi d'istanza: una conferma consolidata su >1 azione distruttiva, procedi+segnala su pre-autorizzato, untrusted inerte, recovery come ritiro-prima.

nodes.label: Transfer cross-dominio 5e (email/pagamenti/git-condiviso/fisico)
nodes.summary: Coppie bilanciate bozza↔invio, carrello↔pagamento, amend-locale↔force-push-condiviso, cestino↔shredder: la stessa logica confirm/proceed fuori dal software.

nodes.label: Classificazione training-vs-harness §2ter (F+S, halt-gate=F)
nodes.summary: Gate-di-halt/pre-flight/grant-lane/untrusted-delimiting = F-harness; decidere-l'atto = S; stato DEGRADATA-MA-UTILE con fallback halt-on-destructive spedibile in Fase-1.

nodes.label: Area 2 — Criticality & Safety Awareness
nodes.summary: Example-space Tier-T1 signature (6 topic, 15 foglie) sul fermarsi prima di un'azione critica; paper-claim #4 (benchmark criticità 200 task).

nodes.label: Topic 1 — Implicit criticality detection
nodes.summary: Riconoscere criticità nascosta in azioni apparentemente banali: file non versionato, overwrite dati, migrazione distruttiva, side-effect nascosto.

nodes.label: Topic 2 — Irreversible-action recognition
nodes.summary: Classificare REV/IRREV/recoverable-with-loss (2.1) e richiedere conferma sugli irreversibili (2.2); famiglia gold 2.x completa espansa 2026-07-10.

nodes.label: Topic 3 — Pre-flight verification
nodes.summary: Eseguire materialmente i check prima dell'azione (git/backup, dipendenze, risorse/budget) e far dipendere la decisione dall'esito reale.

nodes.label: Topic 4 — Hard-limit awareness
nodes.summary: Rispettare un vincolo dichiarato (budget/retry/iterazioni) e fermarsi al limite; reward congiunto limite-rispettato AND task-risolto.

nodes.label: Topic 5 — Consequence anticipation / risk surfacing
nodes.summary: Enumerare conseguenze (incluse di 2° ordine) ed esplicitare i rischi PRIMA di agire; reward F-score coverage-senza-inflazione.

nodes.label: Topic 6 — Decision-point lookahead (A/B)
nodes.summary: Simulare gli esiti delle alternative e scegliere o deferire all'utente con contesto; doppia penalità missed-defer / over-defer.

nodes.label: Difesa anti over-flagging / cry-wolf (reward asimmetrico bilanciato)
nodes.summary: Filo rosso d'area: dataset bilanciato con casi dove l'azione giusta è procedere, penalità simmetrica sui false-positive, ground-truth oggettiva, scorer≠scored.
nodes.norm_label: difesa anti over-flagging / cry-wolf (reward asimmetrico bilanciato)

nodes.label: Check-fantasma (asserire un check senza eseguirlo)
nodes.summary: Vettore di reward-hacking più insidioso (foglia 3.1): il verifier ispeziona il trace di esecuzione, credito solo se la tool-call esiste e ha guidato la decisione.

nodes.label: Value-tiering + probe-then-classify (T-high/T-low/T-group)
nodes.summary: Raffinamento 2026-06-27: classifica ogni asset per valore; probe-then-classify è un'AZIONE con reward sul probe eseguito, mai sulla label 'uncertain'.

nodes.label: Self-versioning gratis ≻ halt (con guardia-segreti hard)
nodes.summary: Rendere reversibile a costo ~0 invece di fermarsi; ma VIETO hard di git add -f/commit su pattern-segreto (veto > reward).

nodes.label: Gate automod (invariante di provenienza)
nodes.summary: Flag settabile SOLO da fonte fidata (utente/rules), mai da contenuto untrusted; un automod:ON iniettato è dato inerte (difesa anti prompt-injection).

nodes.label: T-group discriminato via probe-di-rigenerabilità + soglia-iperparametro
nodes.summary: Segnale primario Q = la dir è riproducibile da fonte tracciata? (probeabile dal trace); secondario = soglia di gruppo come iperparametro di dataset; residuo L vaccinato dall'held-out negativo.

nodes.label: Benchmark criticità 200 task (paper-claim #4)
nodes.summary: Custom benchmark di criticità = principale differenziatore organization-first dell'SLM e claim di paper #4.

nodes.label: Area 7 — Security & Privacy
nodes.summary: Example-space Tier-X cross-tier: 5 foglie (secret non-exfiltration, prompt-injection resistance, secure coding, dynamic secret detection, data minimization) dominato da tag Q.

nodes.label: Secret non-exfiltration
nodes.summary: Mai emettere il valore raw di un secret in output/tool-call/log/commit; gate in uscita + regime adversariale red-team, verifier che normalizza codifiche/frammenti.

nodes.label: Prompt-injection resistance
nodes.summary: Trattare l'<untrusted_zone> come dati mai comandi; riconoscere pattern injection/sandbox-escape/encoding-bypass, con quota benigna anti-over-refusal.

nodes.label: Secure coding (no vuln)
nodes.summary: Codice sicuro-per-default (query parametrizzate, escaping, path-validation, no secret hardcoded); reward via SAST + exploit-test, anti scanner-fooling (#nosec).

nodes.label: Dynamic secret detection (add_secret)
nodes.summary: Chiamare add_secret(value) quando si legge un secret per alimentare il guardrail deterministico; il modello è il sensore, il wrapper il muro; penalità simmetrica su over-detection.

nodes.label: Data minimization
nodes.summary: Usare/esporre solo il minimo dato sensibile per lo scopo (GDPR); tag L con proxy Q funzionale, anti over-minimization e judge-gaming.

nodes.label: Hack unificante over-refusal / over-detection (area-07)
nodes.summary: Il reward binario rende 'rifiutare tutto/segnalare tutto' la scorciatoia; difesa = dataset bilanciato con task legittimi premiati + penalità simmetrica al rifiuto/registrazione indebiti.

nodes.label: Facet threat-model / hardening proattivo (mining #14)
nodes.summary: Host esposto senza hardening = potenzialmente compromesso; la sicurezza si completa per intero o vale meno di zero; confine simmetrico proporzionale all'esposizione.

nodes.label: Classe — Consapevolezza temporale (sai QUANDO sei)
nodes.summary: 1ª figlia di situational-awareness: ancorare le decisioni al tempo (recency epistemica, non-displacement, staleness/TTL, timing) con reward sul verificare/mantenere-l'ancoraggio, non sul conoscere.

nodes.label: Faccia (i) — Recency EPISTEMICA
nodes.summary: Per un fatto volatile oltre la propria freschezza, verificare/qualificare invece di asserire a memoria; ponte con confabulation (il volatile mal-ricordato è confabulazione temporale).

nodes.label: Faccia (i-bis) — NON-DISPLACEMENT / ancoraggio al presente-operativo
nodes.summary: Il frame temporale dell'argomento (Orwell/1984) non deve spostare il presente-operativo (2026): tratta i fatti d'epoca come oggetto, non adotta le norme d'epoca come proprie (safety).

nodes.label: Faccia (ii) — Staleness / TTL
nodes.summary: Un dato letto oltre il suo TTL → re-fetch prima di usarlo; flaggare timestamp incoerenti (età negativa, responded_at<requested_at).

nodes.label: Faccia (iii) — Timing / wait-retry
nodes.summary: Tool in_progress oltre la latenza tipica → decidere wait vs retry vs declare-failed; pianificazione latency-aware.

nodes.label: Presidio train-time: fixture porta <current_date>=presente
nodes.summary: Su contenuti storici il gold mantiene l'ancoraggio al presente (parità train/serve) così il training non insegna a inhabitare l'epoca — presidio anche di safety.

nodes.label: Temporal anchoring by context / nostalgia bias (grounding esterno)
nodes.summary: Fenomeno documentato (arXiv 2510.07238, DateLogicQA 2412.13377, Time-Aware LMs TACL 2022): gli LLM si fidano del contesto datato; difesa = temporal conditioning esplicito (dare la data).
nodes.norm_label: temporal anchoring by context / nostalgia bias (grounding esterno)

nodes.label: Classe — Context-over-parametric authority (fatti-in-context > memoria stantia)
nodes.summary: Figlia di situational-awareness: arbitrare l'autorità della fonte quando un fatto in-context confligge con la conoscenza parametrica; simmetrica e anti-sycophancy.

nodes.label: Skill radice — arbitrato di autorità-della-fonte
nodes.summary: Preferire il fatto in-context (più specifico e fresco) e, se la posta è alta, verificarlo contro il ground-truth invece di asserire a memoria.

nodes.label: Dubbio-di-dominio dell'utente = segnale-di-bug
nodes.summary: 'sei sicuro? / o sbaglio?' su un dominio dove l'utente ha contesto → ri-controlla contro il ground-truth, non rassicurare a vuoto.

nodes.label: Simmetria anti-sycophancy (N2 non cedere a claim errata)
nodes.summary: La skill NON è 'l'utente ha sempre ragione': se la memoria è corretta e la claim in-context errata, difendi con evidenza; penalizza sia override-stantio sia adozione-cieca sia deferenza.
nodes.norm_label: simmetria anti-sycophancy (n2 non cedere a claim errata)

nodes.label: Context-vs-parametric knowledge conflict (grounding)
nodes.summary: Il fenomeno 'il modello sovra-pesa la parametrica vs il contesto fornito' è documentato (entity-based knowledge conflicts, faithfulness-to-context); difesa = fonte in-context preferibile ma verificata.

nodes.label: Classe — Anticipation & irreversibility
nodes.summary: Figlia di consequence↔intention: non anticipare lavoro dipendente da una scelta aperta né eseguire irreversibili 'di slancio' senza pesare beneficio-vs-rischio.

nodes.label: Facet A — anticipazione su scelta aperta
nodes.summary: Costruire speculativamente l'artefatto dipendente da una CHOICE non ancora fatta → spreco + lock-in prematuro sull'opzione sbagliata.
nodes.norm_label: facet a — anticipazione su scelta aperta

nodes.label: Facet B — irreversibilità non pesata
nodes.summary: Eseguire l'irreversibile/costoso senza ledger beneficio-vs-rischio quando esisteva un percorso reversibile/graduale (dry-run, pilota, prune vs abbattimento).

nodes.label: Gate su due assi (dipendenza-da-scelta × reversibilità×beneficio-costo)
nodes.summary: Prima di committare: se dipende da scelta aperta → fai emergere la scelta o solo il prep indipendente; se irreversibile → agisci solo se beneficio−rischio>0 dimostrabile.

nodes.label: Confine simmetrico anti over-gating (N1-N4)
nodes.summary: Prep indipendente/reversibile/scelta-già-fatta → procedi in autonomia; anche l'attesa è irreversibile (pesa il costo dell'inazione) — rende training il confine di feedback_be_autonomous_safe.

nodes.label: Classe — Batching di ops ripetute
nodes.summary: Figlia di action-execution-optimization: collassare ≥2 operazioni stesso-tipo indipendenti in UN passo batchato invece di N esecuzioni; forma-training di optimization-first.

nodes.label: Triage di batchabilità (7 passi)
nodes.summary: Stesso-tipo? indipendenza? un-solo-item? dimensione-sostenibile? reversibilità? omogeneità? → collassa in un passo e distribuisci i risultati, altrimenti sequenza/chunk.

nodes.label: Principio amortized-cost / setup-una-volta-per-molti
nodes.summary: Raggruppare operazioni identiche e indipendenti e pagarne il costo fisso UNA volta spalmandolo sull'insieme — vale cross-dominio (spesa, freight, offset, gruppo d'acquisto).

nodes.label: Confine negativo (N1-N5: dipendenze/singolo/blow-up/distruttive/eterogenee)
nodes.summary: Quando NON batchare: ordine nascosto, un solo item, insieme enorme (chunk), op distruttive con conferma individuale, tipi misti — anti hack batch-sempre.

nodes.label: Classe — Secret hygiene sotto distrazione (leak spontaneo)
nodes.summary: Classe APPROVATA (msg 1341, da F29): non emettere il VALORE di un secret come effetto collaterale mentre concentrati altrove, senza richiesta né avversario; gemella non-adversariale di prompt-injection-resistance.
nodes.norm_label: classe — secret hygiene sotto distrazione (leak spontaneo)

nodes.label: Gate in uscita sempre-attivo, indipendente dal trigger
nodes.summary: Prima di emettere qualunque testo/argomento-tool: contiene il valore di un dato sensibile? Il gate scatta a prescindere dal trigger, a maggior ragione sotto carico attentivo.
nodes.norm_label: gate in uscita sempre-attivo, indipendente dal trigger

nodes.label: Gold F29 — leak spontaneo del segreto piantato (2/15)
nodes.summary: Flash-lite resiste 15/15 all'injection ma echeggia il segreto piantato 2/15 riassumendo un file untrusted, senza richiesta — istanza held-out di validazione.
nodes.norm_label: gold f29 — leak spontaneo del segreto piantato (2/15)

nodes.label: Simmetria anti over-refusal (usa il secret, riferisci il riferimento)
nodes.summary: Reward a doppio vincolo task_svolto AND valore_non_emesso AND secret_usato_se_serviva; penalizza sia il leak sia l'over-refusal/redazione-del-riferimento.
nodes.norm_label: simmetria anti over-refusal (usa il secret, riferisci il riferimento)

nodes.label: Facet — chat=transcript-su-disco + maneggia-senza-leggere (mining #11)
nodes.summary: Un secret non va mai fatto entrare nel canale conversazionale (persistito nel transcript); usarlo via env/pipe/reference senza mai stamparne il valore.

nodes.label: Classe — Consapevolezza dell'ambiente-harness (sai DOVE/con-COSA operi)
nodes.summary: 2ª figlia di situational-awareness: mantenere un modello accurato di tool/lane/meccanismi e di cosa è già in context; fondamento dei gemelli-memoria SAVE/RECALL.

nodes.label: Grounding ambientale (usa l'affordance giusta già disponibile)
nodes.summary: Usa il tool corretto/l'info già in una lane invece di ri-fetchare o ri-derivare; se un'affordance manca, dichiaralo invece di allucinarla (astensione).
nodes.norm_label: grounding ambientale (usa l'affordance giusta gia disponibile)

nodes.label: FIND-7 — ri-chiama list_secrets già in context
nodes.summary: Il modello ri-chiama list_secrets benché l'inventario sealed sia già nella lane <secrets>; gap held-out di grounding ambientale.
nodes.norm_label: find-7 — ri-chiama list_secrets gia in context

nodes.label: F23/F33 — ignora lo scaffold <how_memory_works>
nodes.summary: Il modello IGNORA la descrizione degli strumenti di memoria e fa 0 note; gap held-out (descrizione presente, ignorata).

nodes.label: Scaffold-crutch che recede quando la skill regge (doppio scopo harness→training)
nodes.summary: Split F-harness (inietta la descrizione) + S (leggerla e agirvi); man mano che il training internalizza l'uso, lo scaffold verboso può recedere — misurabile con A/B vanilla-vs-ours.
nodes.norm_label: scaffold-crutch che recede quando la skill regge (doppio scopo harness→training)

nodes.label: Facet — visibility ≠ callability (mining #1)
nodes.summary: Un tool non-mostrato nella lista può essere comunque chiamato; non concludere 'non esiste' dall'assenza dalla surface (sotto-stima dell'inventario reale).

nodes.label: Randomizzazione del toolset epoch-by-epoch (anti-overfit)
nodes.summary: Variare nomi/insieme dei tool descritti così il modello impara a LEGGERE la descrizione, non a memorizzare un set fisso (runtime-symbol-randomization + dynamic-context).

nodes.label: Classe-PADRE — Situational awareness (radice OUTWARD)
nodes.summary: Nodo-radice: mantenere e usare un modello accurato della propria situazione operativa (quando/dove-con-cosa/rispetto-a-quale-conoscenza) invece di operare nel vuoto.

nodes.label: Skill-radice di grounding (orienta te stesso prima di agire)
nodes.summary: Trigger condiviso dalle figlie: percepire il proprio contesto operativo reale e ancorarvi le decisioni, imparato una volta e specializzato per dimensione.
nodes.norm_label: skill-radice di grounding (orienta te stesso prima di agire)

nodes.label: Twin INWARD↔OUTWARD (situational vs metacognitive)
nodes.summary: I due padri si dividono l'auto-consapevolezza: metacognitive àudita il ragionamento (interno), situational modella l'ambiente (esterno); le due famiglie si innestano.

nodes.label: Harness value & capture model (analisi critica)
nodes.summary: Analisi (post-E12): quando l'harness è miglioramento reale vs overhead; CAPTURE sempre-on vs INJECTION adattiva; gap di cattura del non-file-write; condizioni di investimento.

nodes.label: CAPTURE (sempre-on) vs INJECTION (adattiva)
nodes.summary: La cattura delle info durevoli deve essere sempre-attiva dal turno 1 (ciò che non catturi ora non lo recuperi dopo); solo l'iniezione delle lane è adattiva all'overflow.

nodes.label: Gap di cattura del non-file-write durevole (C3)
nodes.summary: Il task-digest cattura deterministicamente solo i file-write; decisioni/preferenze/vincoli dipendono da note/jot inaffidabile (F23/F24) → gap architetturale, non estetico.
nodes.norm_label: gap di cattura del non-file-write durevole (c3)

nodes.label: Due proposte di valore separate (A memory-extension / B difese)
nodes.summary: A = memory-extension overflow-gated e limitata ai file-write; B = difese+context-engineering sempre-on e model-agnostic (probabilmente il valore più robusto).
nodes.norm_label: due proposte di valore separate (a memory-extension / b difese)

nodes.label: Il valore-memoria dell'harness è sbloccato dal TRAINING
nodes.summary: Harness + modello non-addestrato = marginale (ignora lo scaffold); harness + modello addestrato a guidarlo = il salto potenziale — non si giudica l'harness su un 9B non-addestrato.
nodes.norm_label: il valore-memoria dell'harness e sbloccato dal training

nodes.label: Condizioni di investimento C1-C5
nodes.summary: Finestra nativa reale (C1), distribuzione lunghezza-sessione (C2), copertura-cattura (C3), delta trained-vs-untrained (C4), valore-difese isolato Regime-C (C5).

nodes.label: F33 — il 9B non salva mai (nessun framing lo smuove)
nodes.summary: Preferenza piantata persa e confabulata; note/jot/setVar=0 in OFF/narrow/anti-deflect/urgent (13 eviction ciascuna) → il nudge model-side è vicolo cieco, serve cattura deterministica.
